course.title: React Basics: Build Your First Modern Web App
course.subtitle: A Beginner-Friendly React Course with Hands-On Projects and Practical Examples
course.shortDescription: Learn the fundamentals of React from scratch. Build reusable components, understand JSX, props, state, events, and create your first React application in just one hour.
course.fullDescription: Start your React journey with this practical beginner-friendly course. You will learn what React is, why developers love it, and how to build interactive user interfaces using reusable components. Through simple explanations, real-world examples, hands-on coding, quizzes, and a final project, you'll gain the confidence to build your own React applications. No prior React experience is required. By the end of this course, you will have created a small interactive React application and understand the core concepts needed to continue learning advanced React topics.
learningObjectives: Understand what React is and why it is used.
Set up a React development environment.
Build a small interactive React application.
targetAudience: Web developers transitioning from HTML and JavaScript.
Anyone wanting to build interactive web applications.

seo.title: React Basics Course for Beginners | Learn React Step by Step
seo.description: Learn React from scratch with hands-on examples. Master JSX, components, props, state, events, and build your first React project.

thumbnail.title: React Basics
thumbnail.subtitle: Build Your First App
thumbnail.style: Modern tech illustration with bold typography
thumbnail.imagePrompt: Create a premium Udemy course thumbnail for a beginner React course. Large bold title 'React Basics'. Subtitle 'Build Your First App'. Modern blue and dark theme with glowing React logo, laptop displaying code, floating UI cards, clean typography, high contrast, minimal distractions, professional educational style, 16:9 composition.

promoVideo.script: Welcome! Have you ever wanted to build modern websites with React? In this beginner-friendly course, you'll learn React step by step using simple English and practical examples. We'll cover JSX, components, props, state, events, and build a real mini project together. No React experience is needed. By the end of this course, you'll understand the core concepts and be ready to build your own applications. Let's start coding together!
promoVideo.imagePrompt: Modern developer workspace with React logo, coding editor, vibrant blue lighting, friendly instructor atmosphere, cinematic educational scene.

sections.title: Getting Started with React
sections.description: Learn the basics of React and prepare your development environment.

lectures.title: Introduction to React
lectures.summary: Learn what React is and why developers use it.
lectures.script: Welcome to the course. React is a JavaScript library used to build user interfaces. Instead of creating large HTML pages manually, React lets us build reusable components. Companies like Facebook and many startups use React because it is fast, flexible, and easy to maintain. Throughout this course, we will build small examples to understand every concept.
lectures.demo: Show a static HTML page, then the same page built with React components.
lectures.exercise: Search three popular websites that use React.

quiz.question: What is React mainly used for?

video.thumbnailPrompt: React introduction with glowing React logo and code editor
video.imagePrompt: Modern coding workspace with React logo and code editor
video.videoPrompt: Educational coding tutorial introducing React fundamentals.

lectures.title: Setting Up React
lectures.summary: Install Node.js and create a React project using Vite.
lectures.script: Install Node.js. Open your terminal. Use npm create vite to generate a React project. Install dependencies and run the development server. Open the project in Visual Studio Code and explore the folder structure.
lectures.demo: Create a new Vite React project.
lectures.exercise: Install Node.js and successfully run your first React application.

video.imagePrompt: Terminal and VS Code with React project
video.videoPrompt: Guide viewers through creating a React project.

sections.title: Core React Concepts
sections.description: Understand JSX, components, props, state, and events.

lectures.title: JSX and Components
lectures.summary: Learn JSX syntax and reusable components.
lectures.script: JSX allows us to write HTML-like syntax inside JavaScript. Components are reusable pieces of UI. We'll create a Header component and reuse it in our application.

video.videoPrompt: Explain JSX and components with live coding.

lectures.title: Props and State
lectures.script: Props allow parent components to send data to child components. State stores information that changes over time. We'll use the useState hook to build a simple counter.
lectures.exercise: Display your name using props and create a click counter.

video.videoPrompt: Build a React counter using useState.

lectures.title: Handling Events and Lists
lectures.summary: Respond to user actions and display collections.
lectures.script: React makes event handling simple using onClick and other event handlers. We can also render lists using map and assign unique keys.
lectures.exercise: Display five favorite fruits from an array.

quiz.question: Which JavaScript function is commonly used to render lists?

video.videoPrompt: Teach event handling and list rendering.

sections.title: Mini Project
sections.description: Build a complete beginner React application.

lectures.title: Build a Simple Profile Card App
lectures.summary: Combine everything learned into one project.
lectures.script: Create reusable components for ProfileCard, Button, and App. Pass data using props. Use state to track likes. Handle button clicks and display dynamic information.
lectures.demo: Build a profile card with Like button.
lectures.exercise: Customize the profile with your own information and add another button.

quiz.question: What concept updates the like counter?

video.videoPrompt: Build a complete beginner React project from scratch.

finalProject.title: Personal Profile Card
finalProject.description: Build a responsive React profile card using components, props, state, and event handling.

certificateProject.title: React Beginner Portfolio Project
certificateProject.description: Submit a completed React profile card application demonstrating components, props, state, and events.

faq.question: Do I need JavaScript before learning React?
faq.answer: Yes. Basic JavaScript knowledge is recommended.

faq.question: Is this course suitable for complete beginners?
faq.answer: Yes. Everything is explained step by step.

faq.question: Will I build a project?
faq.answer: Yes. You'll build a practical React profile card application.

faq.question: What should I learn after this course?
faq.answer: React Router, Hooks, Forms, APIs, Context API, and state management.
